title: Tokenizer Inference Load and Run
interaction_flow: Caller → load_model(jit_filepath, tokenizer_config=None, device): entry point
Caller → load_model(jit_filepath, tokenizer_config, device): with config
_load_pytorch_model → tokenizer_config['name']: extract tokenizer_name (e.g. CI, DI, CV, DV)
load_encoder_model → _load_pytorch_model(jit_filepath, tokenizer_config, device): same as above
Caller → ImageTokenizer.__init__(checkpoint, checkpoint_enc, checkpoint_dec, tokenizer_config, device, dtype)
ImageTokenizer → return latent (CI: Bx16xhxw) or (DI: indices Bxhxw + code Bx6xhxw)
ImageTokenizer → pad_image_batch(image): pad to _SPATIAL_ALIGN=16, get crop_region
ImageTokenizer → numpy2tensor(padded_input_image, dtype, device): convert to Bx3xHxW float
ImageTokenizer → tensor2numpy(output_tensor): convert back to BxHxWxC uint8
ImageTokenizer → unpad_image_batch(padded_output_image, crop_region): crop to original size
TokenizerModel.__init__(config): instantiates network, loss, metric via instantiate()
key_relationships: load_model depends_on load_jit_model (JIT path) and _load_pytorch_model (PyTorch path)
TokenizerModels enum maps: CI→ContinuousImageTokenizer, DI→DiscreteImageTokenizer, CV→CausalContinuousVideoTokenizer, DV→CausalDiscreteVideoTokenizer
TokenizerModel extends Model; composes network, loss, metric, optional EMA tracker
TokenizerModel.state_dict filters out loss and patcher keys (not saved in checkpoint)
branching_logic: load_model: if tokenizer_config is None → JIT path; else → PyTorch path
load_encoder_model: same JIT vs PyTorch branch; PyTorch path extracts encoder_jit()
load_decoder_model: same JIT vs PyTorch branch; PyTorch path extracts decoder_jit()
ImageTokenizer.autoencode: if _full_model is not None → single pass; else → encode+decode
ImageTokenizer.encode: if output is Tensor → return as-is; else → return output[:-1] (strip last element)
TokenizerModel.get_input_key: checks image_key then video_key in data_batch
important_notes: JIT path: loads pre-compiled ScriptModule directly via torch.jit.load
PyTorch path: constructs fresh network, loads weights from JIT checkpoint via state_dict transfer
encoder_jit() and decoder_jit() are methods on the tokenizer network classes
load_state_dict is overridden in TokenizerModel to filter only matching keys (avoids missing key errors for loss/patcher params)
TokenizerModel.state_dict explicitly excludes: loss.*, ema.loss-*, network.encoder.patcher.*, network.decoder.unpatcher.*
EMA (Exponential Moving Average) optional: enabled via config.ema.enabled, uses EMAModelTracker
